title: BWSSB Announces 3% Annual Tariff Escalation Effective April 2026
summary: BWSSB has implemented the annual 3% escalation on all water tariff slabs as approved by the Karnataka Government. Domestic first slab rises from ₹9.27 to ₹9.53 per KL.
aiSummary: Your water bill will go up slightly from April 2026. The first 8 KL of water will now cost ₹9.53 per KL instead of ₹9.27. This is a routine 3% annual increase approved by the government.
citizenImpact: Monthly bills will increase by 3% from April 2026 onwards.

title: Mandatory Rainwater Harvesting (RWH) for Plots Above 1,200 sq.m
summary: BWSSB mandates Rainwater Harvesting structures for all sites with ground area above 1,200 sq.m (approx. 12,917 sq.ft). Non-compliance will attract a 50% surcharge on total water bill.
aiSummary: If your property or apartment building has a total ground area above 1,200 square meters, you must install a Rainwater Harvesting system. If you don't, BWSSB will add a 50% penalty on top of your entire water bill.
citizenImpact: Large plot owners and apartment associations must install RWH or face 50% bill surcharge.

title: BWSSB Scheduled Maintenance: North Bengaluru Zones — July 24-25, 2026
summary: Due to pipeline repair works at Hesaraghatta Road, water supply will be interrupted in Yeshwanthpur, Rajajinagar, and Malleswaram zones from 6 AM July 24 to 6 AM July 25, 2026.
aiSummary: No water for 24 hours! If you live in Yeshwanthpur, Rajajinagar, or Malleswaram, please store water before July 24, 6 AM. Supply will resume by July 25, 6 AM.
citizenImpact: Store water before July 24, 2026 at 6 AM. Supply resumes July 25 at 6 AM.

title: Online Billing & Payment Services — BWSSB CMS Portal Upgraded
summary: BWSSB Consumer Management System (CMS) at cms.bwssb.gov.in has been upgraded. Citizens can now view bills, pay online, and submit complaints from a single portal.
aiSummary: BWSSB's online portal has been upgraded. You can now check your bill, pay online, and file complaints all in one place at cms.bwssb.gov.in without visiting the office.
citizenImpact: You can now manage your water connection fully online.

title: BWSSB Water Quality Test Report — Q1 2026 (Jan–Mar)
summary: BWSSB has released Q1 2026 water quality test results for all supply zones. Cauvery water supply meets WHO and BIS standards for all tested parameters.
aiSummary: Good news! BWSSB tested tap water quality across Bengaluru from January to March 2026. All areas passed the safety standards. The water supplied is safe to drink after basic filtration.
citizenImpact: Bengaluru tap water has passed all Q1 2026 safety tests.

title: BWSSB New Connection — Aadhaar-Based Application Process Launched
summary: New water connection applications can now be submitted online using Aadhaar-based eKYC. Physical visits to BWSSB offices are no longer required for the initial application.
aiSummary: Need a new water connection? You can now apply online using your Aadhaar card — no need to visit the BWSSB office. The application is processed digitally within 15 working days.
citizenImpact: Apply for a new water connection online using Aadhaar — no office visit needed.
